tasks.title: Add Aegis Closeout Gate and Live-Agent Completion Flow
tasks.description: Implement a project-local Aegis closeout command plus Claude runtime instructions and gates so an agent cannot treat portable Aegis work as complete until readiness, tracking, plan, verification, evidence, handoff, and optional Git guidance checks all pass mechanically.
tasks.details:
Codebase analysis summary: Aegis is a Python 3.11 package defined in pyproject.toml with the console entry point aegis = aegis_foundation.cli:main and repository wrapper support in scripts/codex-task. Core installer/runtime behavior lives in scripts/_aegis_installer.py and is packaged under aegis_foundation/assets/scripts/_aegis_installer.py. Existing commands include inspect, plan-install, status, install, verify --strict, certify-release, kickoff, and log in aegis_foundation/cli.py, with matching scripts/codex-task aegis handlers. Kickoff/log state is stored under .aegis/state/current-work.json and .aegis/state/pending-tracking.json, workflow files are scaffolded from aegis_foundation/assets/templates/aegis/workflow/{session,plan,tracker,findings,decisions,implementation,changelog,handoff}.md, and Claude hook behavior is enforced through .claude/scripts/gate_lib.py plus packaged copies under aegis_foundation/assets/.claude/scripts/. Current strict verification in scripts/_aegis_installer.py validates manifest, local shim, workflow templates, current work, branch alignment, tracking surfaces, pending tracking, Claude hook registration, protected path guards, and optional Taskmaster/Serena integration state. Existing regression coverage is concentrated in tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py, tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py, and tests/claude_adapter/test_*.

Implement the closeout flow in the shared Aegis core, not separately per adapter. Add a closeout function in scripts/_aegis_installer.py, for example closeout(target_dir, *, source_root, require_clean_git=False, commit_guidance=True), and expose it through aegis_foundation/cli.py as aegis closeout --target-dir . plus scripts/codex-task aegis closeout. If MCP already exposes strict verify as an optional flag, consider adding aegis.closeout only if it fits the existing aegis_mcp.server V1 tool schema and acknowledgement conventions; otherwise keep MCP follow-up explicit and document the CLI as the portable authority.

The closeout command should be read-mostly but may write a closeout report and, if all gates pass, update Aegis-owned workflow state. It must run or directly invoke the same readiness logic used by .claude/scripts/readiness.sh and require READY. It must require .aegis/state/current-work.json to exist and represent active in-progress work. It must fail if .aegis/state/pending-tracking.json contains events or invalid JSON. It must enforce required plan steps in order: plan-step-scope completed before implementation, plan-step-implement completed after a task-scoped mutation evidence entry, and plan-step-verify completed only after strict verification evidence is logged. It must run strict verification through the existing verify(..., strict=True) core path or the installed local shim and require status passed. It must require current plan and tracker agreement for required steps rather than trusting only one file.

Add evidence cross-reference checks. For each required evidence item, especially task-scoped source changes and verification commands/reports, verify that the evidence token appears in sessions/current, the active TRACKER.md, IMPLEMENTATION.md, CHANGELOG.md, HANDOFF.md, and plans/current. Reuse existing S:W:H:E token conventions from log_work: [S:<date>|W:task<id>-<slug>|H:<handler>|E:<evidence>]. Closeout should detect missing surfaces with precise check IDs such as closeout.evidence.session, closeout.evidence.tracker, closeout.evidence.implementation, closeout.evidence.changelog, closeout.evidence.handoff, and closeout.evidence.plan. It should not count Serena memory or Taskmaster entries as required evidence unless current-work explicitly marks those integrations required; Taskmaster and Serena remain optional.

Improve handoff semantics. The current HANDOFF.md template has Current State, What Was Done, Current Issues/Blockers, Next Steps, Important Context, and Progress Log sections, while aegis log currently appends progress entries. Add closeout validation that HANDOFF.md contains semantic current state and next steps that reflect the completed task rather than only appended logs. Implement a conservative parser/checker that requires non-placeholder content under Current State and Next Steps, requires the changed source evidence and strict verification evidence to be mentioned outside Progress Log, and rejects kickoff-only default text such as 'Task <id> has been kicked off through Aegis' as the final current state. Consider adding an aegis handoff-update or closeout --update-handoff option only if it can update Aegis-owned handoff sections deterministically; otherwise fail with actionable remediation telling the agent to use aegis log plus a handoff update before closeout.

Update Claude instructions and hooks so completion cannot remain advisory. Extend the installed Claude runtime entrypoint rendered by _render_claude_entrypoint in scripts/_aegis_installer.py, the packaged assets, and docs/aegis/invocation-contract.md to define the normal loop as readiness -> scope log -> scoped mutation -> pending tracking -> implementation log -> verification log -> strict verify -> closeout. Add .claude/commands/closeout.md and package it into installed targets if command files are managed for Claude. Update .claude/settings.json allowlist rendering to permit aegis closeout and ./.aegis/bin/aegis closeout. If a Stop hook can mechanically nudge or block final-task-complete phrasing, add a narrow closeout gate script or extend gate_lib.py with a Stop-time check that blocks when the agent attempts to end while plan-step-verify is complete but closeout has not passed; avoid blocking ordinary pauses that still have legitimate next steps. Any hook changes must be mirrored in root .claude/scripts and aegis_foundation/assets/.claude/scripts, and strict verification must validate the new hook/command registration where appropriate.

Produce a machine-readable report at .aegis/reports/closeout-report.json with schema_version, status, closed_at or checked_at, target_root, current_work task, readiness result, strict_verify report path/status, plan step statuses, evidence matrix by surface, handoff semantic checks, pending tracking summary, optional integration summary, git guidance, and failures. On success, consider updating current-work status to closed or closeout-passed while preserving enough state for strict verify and future inspection; if changing status from in-progress would break readiness or strict verify, record closeout_passed_at and closeout_report instead of changing the active status until a separate archive task exists.

Add optional Git/PR guidance without defaulting to gac. Align with templates/handlers/operators/git/create-commit-message.md and templates/shared/tools/tool-selection-matrix.md: recommend normal git add, git commit, git push, and gh pr create when the user delegates Git work and auth is available; mention gac only as an explicit user-requested legacy/manual convenience. Closeout should print or report suggested commands, not run commits or PR creation by default. Guard/docs should reject any new Aegis closeout guidance that makes gac the default.

Keep implementation portable. The closeout command must work in installed target projects that have no .taskmaster and no .serena directories. It may report those integrations as detected/absent and required/optional based on .aegis/state/current-work.json, following the same semantics already used by readiness and strict verification. Do not add mandatory Taskmaster set-status or Serena memory writes to the portable Aegis closeout path.
tasks.testStrategy:
Extend existing pytest suites instead of creating an unrelated harness. Add parser and CLI wiring tests in tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py for aegis closeout and scripts/codex-task aegis closeout. Add core closeout tests that install Aegis into a tmp target, initialize git, run kickoff, verify readiness READY, and assert closeout fails before scope, implementation, verification, strict verify, handoff semantic, and pending-tracking requirements are satisfied. Each failure should assert a precise failed check ID and nonzero CLI exit.

Add a live-agent-like end-to-end regression using tests/fixtures/aegis-target-projects/web-started, modeled on test_installed_web_target_real_feature_change_updates_full_workflow in tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py. The test should copy the fixture, install Aegis with Claude enabled, run kickoff for an Add Cart Button task without Taskmaster or Serena, confirm readiness READY, log scope with plan-step-scope completed, mutate src/main.ts to add a visible Add to cart button, simulate PostToolUse tracking, prove a second mutation and Stop are blocked until aegis log clears the pending source evidence, log implementation with plan-step-implement completed, run a real source-inspection verification command, log verification with plan-step-verify completed, run ./.aegis/bin/aegis verify --strict, update HANDOFF.md with semantic Current State and Next Steps, then run ./.aegis/bin/aegis closeout and assert status passed. Validate that source and verification evidence tokens appear in sessions/current, TRACKER.md, IMPLEMENTATION.md, CHANGELOG.md, HANDOFF.md, and plans/current, and that .aegis/reports/closeout-report.json records the same matrix.

Add negative installed-target regressions: closeout fails when readiness is BLOCKED; closeout fails when .aegis/state/pending-tracking.json contains an event; closeout fails when plan-step-verify is completed before plan-step-implement; closeout fails when strict verify has not been run or currently fails; closeout fails when source evidence is missing from one required surface; closeout fails when HANDOFF.md only has kickoff/default state plus appended Progress Log entries; closeout still passes in a target with no .taskmaster and no .serena; closeout fails only when current-work marks Taskmaster or Serena required and the integration is absent or invalid.

Add Claude adapter/asset tests in tests/claude_adapter/test_adapter_contract_files.py or tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py to assert installed CLAUDE.md or command assets instruct agents to run aegis closeout before declaring completion, settings allow aegis closeout and ./.aegis/bin/aegis closeout, and hook registration remains intact. Add guard tests where appropriate to ensure commit/PR guidance says normal git commands are the default and does not introduce default gac wording.

Run targeted verification commands before closing this task: python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py tests/claude_adapter; python3 scripts/codex-guard validate --include-untracked; git diff --check. If MCP closeout support is added, also run the focused MCP server/schema tests. Capture command evidence under the Task 117 reports folder and ensure the final closeout report path is referenced from session, TRACKER, IMPLEMENTATION, CHANGELOG, HANDOFF, and plans/current.

tasks.title: Native Global MCP Bootstrap for Aegis
tasks.description: Make Aegis registerable from any fresh project through native Claude and Codex MCP client commands, with deterministic command generation, optional execution, verification, and documentation for package, pinned, GitHub, local wheel, and source checkout install modes.
tasks.details: Codebase context: Aegis is already packaged in `pyproject.toml` as `aegis-foundation` with console scripts `aegis` and `aegis-mcp-server`; package CLI parsing lives in `aegis_foundation/cli.py`; the repository-local wrapper mirrors Aegis commands in `scripts/codex-task`; deterministic runtime behavior lives in `scripts/_aegis_installer.py`; MCP server startup and discovery surfaces live in `aegis_mcp/server.py` through `AegisMCPConfig`, `V1_TOOL_NAMES`, `RESOURCE_URIS`, `PROMPT_NAMES`, `--default-target-dir`, `--transport`, and `--describe-config`; release docs currently show manual `.mcp.json` / Codex config snippets in `docs/aegis/mcp-client-setup.md` and `docs/aegis/distribution.md`, mirrored into `aegis_foundation/assets/docs/aegis/`; release and MCP coverage already exists in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py`, `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_server.py`, `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_invocation_contract.py`, and `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py`. Implement a native MCP registration layer as the default bootstrap path. Add a deterministic registration module, preferably a small package module such as `aegis_foundation/mcp_registration.py` or a shared `scripts/_aegis_mcp_registration.py` if keeping the current script-core pattern is more consistent, with typed client specs for `claude` and `codex`, install source modes for unpinned package (`aegis-foundation`), pinned package (`aegis-foundation==<version>`), GitHub URL or ref, local wheel path, and source checkout path, plus stable JSON output containing client, scope, server name `aegis`, package spec, generated argv, rendered command, target dir, transport, and safety notes. Generate native command argv, not shell fragments, for examples including `claude mcp add --scope user aegis -- uvx --from aegis-foundation aegis-mcp-server --default-target-dir . --transport stdio`, `claude mcp add --scope project aegis -- uvx --from aegis-foundation aegis-mcp-server --default-target-dir . --transport stdio`, and `codex mcp add aegis -- uvx --from aegis-foundation aegis-mcp-server --default-target-dir . --transport stdio`. Preserve the existing MCP server command shape from `aegis_mcp/server.py`; only the client registration wrapper changes. Add package CLI subcommands in `aegis_foundation/cli.py`, and matching repo-local support in `scripts/codex-task`, for generate, execute, and verify flows, for example `aegis mcp generate-registration`, `aegis mcp execute-registration`, and `aegis mcp verify-registration` or an equivalent argparse shape that matches existing hyphenated command style. Generate mode must be read-only and print JSON by default, with an optional command rendering suitable for docs. Execute mode must locate the requested native client with `shutil.which`, run `subprocess.run` with the exact argv list, capture stdout/stderr/returncode into JSON, and never use `shell=True`. Verify mode must use native client inspection commands for Claude and Codex, parse their stdout/stderr robustly enough to recognize the `aegis` server, the `uvx --from ... aegis-mcp-server` command, `--default-target-dir`, and `--transport stdio`, and return structured pass/fail checks; add an optional stdio probe that launches the generated MCP server command and verifies discovery of `aegis.*` tools using the existing MCP client patterns from release tests. Missing client binaries must fail with a clear JSON error such as `status: missing_client` and include the command that would have been run. Make direct config-file writes an explicit fallback only: add a flag such as `--write-config-fallback` or `--fallback-config-file` for legacy/manual flows, keep it off by default, and ensure docs state native client registration is preferred. Ensure generated commands work without cloning `/home/loucmane/codex`: default to package assets, keep source checkout mode explicit, and verify `aegis-mcp-server --describe-config` reports `asset_origin: package` unless a source path was requested. Update docs in both `docs/aegis/` and packaged assets for global Claude, project Claude, Codex, pinned package, GitHub URL/ref, local wheel, and source checkout flows; replace manual `.mcp.json` as the primary path with native `claude mcp add` / `codex mcp add`, while retaining manual config snippets only under a fallback section. Update the release verification matrix to include native client registration, verification parsing, missing-client behavior, and fresh-project bootstrap acceptance. Acceptance: from a fresh directory with no checkout, a user can run the generated native client registration command globally or per-project, the native client can discover the Aegis MCP server and `aegis.*` tools, and the registered tool path can install packaged runtime assets and then run `aegis.kickoff`, `aegis.log`, `aegis.verify`, and `aegis.closeout` without requiring this repository to exist locally.
tasks.testStrategy: Add focused unit and integration coverage, likely in a new `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py`, plus documentation assertions in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py`. Test command generation for each client/scope/source mode: Claude global user scope, Claude project scope, Codex default registration, unpinned package, pinned package, GitHub URL/ref, local wheel, and source checkout. Assert generated argv exactly matches native client semantics and includes `uvx`, `--from`, the selected package or artifact spec, `aegis-mcp-server`, `--default-target-dir`, the requested target, `--transport`, and `stdio`; assert rendered examples include the required commands from the task request. Test execute mode by monkeypatching `shutil.which` and `subprocess.run`; assert subprocess receives the expected argv list, cwd, environment, text capture settings, no shell invocation, and that stdout/stderr/returncode are surfaced in structured JSON. Test missing native clients for both `claude` and `codex` by forcing `shutil.which` to return `None`; assert nonzero status, no subprocess call, actionable error text, and generated command fallback in the payload. Test verify-mode parsing with fixture outputs for successful Claude registration, successful Codex registration, missing `aegis`, wrong package spec, wrong transport, and malformed/native-client error output. Add a fresh-project acceptance test using a temporary directory and fake native client binaries on PATH that record `mcp add` invocations and return realistic `mcp list` or `mcp get` output; then run the generated MCP command or an optional stdio probe to confirm discovery of `V1_TOOL_NAMES`, including `aegis.inspect`, `aegis.install`, `aegis.verify`, `aegis.kickoff`, `aegis.log`, and `aegis.closeout` where available. Reuse existing local wheel smoke patterns from `test_aegis_release_distribution.py` for optional environment-gated tests that build a wheel and run `uvx --from <wheel> aegis-mcp-server --default-target-dir . --transport stdio` outside the repo. Add docs tests asserting `docs/aegis/mcp-client-setup.md`, its packaged asset copy, `docs/aegis/distribution.md`, and `docs/aegis/release-verification-matrix.md` cover native Claude global, Claude project, Codex, pinned package, GitHub URL, local wheel, source checkout, fallback config-file write, and no-clone acceptance language. Run focused tests: `python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_server.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_invocation_contract.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py`. Final workflow gates should include `python3 scripts/codex-task taskmaster health`, `python3 scripts/codex-task plan sync`, `python3 scripts/codex-task work-tracking audit`, `python3 scripts/codex-guard validate --include-untracked`, and `git diff --check`.

tasks.title: Prove Local-First Aegis Package and MCP Installation Before PyPI
tasks.description: Build and validate local wheel and sdist artifacts for `aegis-foundation`, then prove a fresh project can register and run the Aegis MCP workflow from only the local artifact or package-style local project path. This task makes local artifact installation the required gate before any TestPyPI or PyPI publishing work.
tasks.details:
Codebase analysis summary: `pyproject.toml` already defines the Python 3.11 distribution `aegis-foundation` with console scripts `aegis = aegis_foundation.cli:main` and `aegis-mcp-server = aegis_mcp.server:main`; `MANIFEST.in` and `[tool.setuptools.package-data]` include `aegis_foundation/assets/**`. The deterministic installer/runtime source of truth is `scripts/_aegis_installer.py`, and package invocation delegates through `aegis_foundation/cli.py`. MCP startup and tools live in `aegis_mcp/server.py` via `AegisMCPConfig`, `V1_TOOL_NAMES`, `RESOURCE_URIS`, and `PROMPT_NAMES`. Native MCP registration support lives in `aegis_foundation/mcp_registration.py` with package, pinned, GitHub, wheel, and source modes, surfaced through `aegis mcp generate-registration`, `execute-registration`, and `verify-registration`, with mirrored repo-local argparse support in `scripts/codex-task`. Existing tests already cover cross-project installs (`tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_cross_project_smoke.py`), invocation contracts (`test_aegis_invocation_contract.py`), release distribution metadata and env-gated local wheel smokes (`test_aegis_release_distribution.py`), native MCP registration (`test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py`), and MCP target flows (`test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py`). The remaining gap is a mandatory, fresh-folder, local-artifact proof that exercises registration plus the full workflow through closeout without depending on `/home/loucmane/codex`, `AEGIS_SOURCE_ROOT`, repository-local `scripts/codex-task`, `.taskmaster/`, or `.serena/`.

Implementation approach:
1. Create normal Task 119 work-tracking artifacts and reports under the current repository workflow before changing code. Record the local-first release decision: no TestPyPI/PyPI publishing task may start until the local artifact proof report passes.
2. Harden local artifact production. Use the existing `aegis certify-release` path in `scripts/_aegis_installer.py` as the preferred release-candidate authority. Ensure it builds both wheel and sdist using `uv build --sdist --wheel --out-dir <dist>` when `uv` is available, falls back to `python -m build` only when appropriate, computes SHA-256 checksums, inspects wheel/sdist contents for required package assets, entry points, schemas, MCP server package, workflow templates, Claude gate scripts, and packaged docs, and writes durable evidence to a report such as `reports/aegis-local-install-proof/local-artifact-proof.json` or the existing release certification report path if that is the better local pattern.
3. Make local artifact and local package-path MCP registration first-class. In `aegis_foundation/mcp_registration.py`, verify that `--source-mode wheel --artifact <wheel>` and the local project/path mode generate `uvx --from <artifact-or-path> aegis-mcp-server --default-target-dir . --transport stdio` without requiring `--source-root` or a repository checkout. Add path validation and structured errors for missing wheel, missing local project `pyproject.toml`, unsupported archive type, or artifact paths that resolve outside the caller-visible filesystem. Preserve argv-list execution with `subprocess.run(..., shell=False)` and keep manual config-file writes fallback-only.
4. Add a dedicated fresh-folder proof harness, likely `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_local_artifact_mcp_proof.py`. The harness should build a local wheel/sdist into a temporary dist directory, create a brand-new temporary git project, clear `AEGIS_SOURCE_ROOT` and source-checkout `PYTHONPATH` leakage, register Aegis MCP using the native registration command generated from the local wheel or package path, start the MCP server through the generated `mcp_server_argv` with `mcp.client.stdio`, and prove the server reports packaged assets via `aegis-mcp-server --describe-config` or equivalent structured config evidence.
5. Exercise the complete workflow from the fresh target through MCP and/or the installed artifact command only: call `aegis.inspect`, `aegis.status`, `aegis.plan_install`, `aegis.install` with explicit apply, `aegis.kickoff`, `aegis.log` for scope evidence, make a small task-scoped source or docs change in the new git project, record S:W:H:E tracking with `aegis.log`, run `aegis.verify` including strict verification when closeout requires it, log verification evidence, then call `aegis.closeout` with the required acknowledgement/update-handoff semantics. Assert that `.aegis/foundation-manifest.json`, `.aegis/state/current-work.json`, `.aegis/reports/install-plan.json`, `.aegis/reports/install-report.json`, `.aegis/reports/verification-report.json`, and `.aegis/reports/closeout-report.json` exist and show passing status.
6. Prove no checkout dependence. After the fresh-folder proof, recursively scan target `.aegis/` state, reports, generated contract files, current session, current plan, and work-tracking surfaces to ensure they do not contain `/home/loucmane/codex` or any source checkout path unless the specific test is intentionally exercising local project-path mode and the path appears only in the native client registration command evidence, not in installed target runtime state.
7. Keep fake-client and live-client layers separate. Use deterministic fake `claude` and `codex` binaries in tests to prove native registration execution and verification without requiring real clients on CI. Add an optional live smoke, gated by explicit environment variables, that runs the same local wheel registration against real installed native clients when available; the local artifact proof itself should still be runnable on a developer machine without publishing anything.
8. Update docs and packaged doc mirrors in `docs/aegis/distribution.md`, `docs/aegis/mcp-client-setup.md`, `docs/aegis/release-policy.md`, `docs/aegis/release-verification-matrix.md`, and `aegis_foundation/assets/docs/aegis/*`. Document exact pre-publication commands for local wheel/sdist build, local wheel MCP registration, local project-path MCP registration, fresh-folder install/kickoff/log/verify/closeout, expected report locations, and the rule that TestPyPI/PyPI publishing is blocked until this proof passes. Keep public package snippets present but clearly downstream of this local proof.
9. Update CI/release templates only as documentation unless the repository already has a concrete CI workflow for Aegis release gates. If adding a command or report flag, prefer extending `aegis certify-release` rather than creating a separate release engine.
10. Preserve existing compatibility: do not break `scripts/codex-task aegis ...`, existing package CLI commands, existing MCP tool names, generated `.aegis/` paths, existing env-gated wheel smoke tests, or Task 111/115/117 target fixtures. The task is complete only when a brand-new temporary project can use the built local artifact plus the generated native registration command to complete inspect, install, kickoff, S:W:H:E log, verify, and closeout without cloning or invoking this repository checkout.
tasks.testStrategy:
Required focused verification:
